stories.headline: April DeFi exploits hit $620M as Kelp DAO contagion drains $6.4B from TVL
stories.tldr: April crypto exploits crossed $620M after Kelp DAO's $292M rsETH bridge drain dragged total DeFi TVL from $26.4B to roughly $20B.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Cross-chain bridge risk is now a systemic liquidity threat, not a per-protocol bug — every wrapped-asset position is suddenly priced with a discount.
the_details: Kelp DAO lost 116,500 rsETH (~$292M, 18% of supply) through its LayerZero bridge on April 19.
Volo Protocol followed days later with a $3.5M exploit, plus Dango ($410K) and Silo Finance ($392K) earlier in the month.
DeFi TVL fell from $26.4B (April 18) to ~$20B by April 27, a $6.4B liquidity exodus.

stories.headline: North Korea pinned to Drift's $285M April Fool's hack via pre-signed authorizations
stories.tldr: Authorities suspect North Korean operators drained $285M from Solana perp DEX Drift on April 1 using long-running social engineering.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Admin-key compromises now eclipse smart-contract bugs as the dominant DeFi attack vector — the threat model is HR, not Solidity.
the_details: Drift attackers used pre-signed hidden authorizations planted via a months-long social engineering campaign.
Stolen assets included USDC, SOL, JLP and WBTC, drained in a single coordinated sequence.
Combined with Kelp DAO, the two attacks make April 2026 the worst DeFi security month on record.

stories.headline: Iran peace proposal flips crypto Fear & Greed to 47 from 33 last week
stories.tldr: Crypto sentiment swung from fear to neutral in seven days after Tehran sent Washington a fresh peace proposal over the weekend.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Crypto is trading as a pure macro-risk asset — geopolitics and the Fed meeting outweigh any on-chain catalyst this week.
the_details: Fear & Greed Index rose to 47 (Neutral) from 33 yesterday and 29 a week ago.
Total crypto market cap climbed 1.7% to $2.71T on $66.6B in 24h volume.
WTI crude still above $90/bbl and Brent above $100 keep an inflation lid on the rebound.

stories.headline: Solana ETFs pull $9.4M weekly inflows as Western Union taps SOL for payments
stories.tldr: Spot SOL ETFs added $9.44M last week and Western Union expanded onto Solana, signaling institutional flow widening past BTC and ETH.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A Tier-1 remittance rail running on Solana is the kind of distribution validation altcoin ETFs need to pull real allocator money.
the_details: BTC ETFs led with $823.7M and ETH ETFs added $155M last week — SOL is now firmly in the inflow club.
GSR's new fund added SOL allocations; Ramp added SOL conversion support.
Western Union's Solana expansion follows a $1.2B weekly ETP inflow streak across crypto products.

stories.headline: Bhutan moves another 250 BTC; 2026 sovereign outflows top $240M
stories.tldr: Bhutan transferred 250 BTC on April 13, pushing 2026 sovereign outflows past $240M with holdings down 73% from the October 2024 peak.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The state-actor selling everyone wrote off as a niche curiosity is steady, escalating overhead supply that ETFs are quietly absorbing.
the_details: Bhutan's 2026 BTC outflows now total $240M per Arkham wallet tracking.
Total holdings have fallen 73% from the October 2024 peak of roughly 13,000 BTC.
Selling coincides with BTC stalling at the $80K resistance — supply absorption explains why $5B in fresh USDT can't push price.
